steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure around the blank
steps.content:
Left side: noun phrase (the emission of light with modifiers) serving as the subject.
Right side: noun phrase (the element iron as the source of the light) serving as the direct object.
The structure is: [subject] [blank] [object] — a clause in need of a verb.

steps.title: Apply the finite verb rule
steps.content:
A clause must have a finite verb as its predicate. Since the subject “emission” is singular, the verb must be singular as well. “Identifies” is the only option providing a singular finite verb.

The others are non-finite forms (present participle, infinitive, and perfect participle) that cannot serve as a clause's main predicate.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
